from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    The function now groups statements by the same subject to produce a more compact,
    natural‑sounding description.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a textual fragment. The placeholder {subj} and {obj} will be filled.
    verb_map = {
        "league": "plays in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "is managed by {obj}",
        "position": "holds the position of {obj}",
        "ground": "plays at {obj}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "club": "plays for {obj}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {obj}",
        "height": "has a height of {obj} meters",
        "youthclub": "started at youth club {obj}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {obj}",
        "weight": "weighs {obj} kilograms",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his active years in {obj}",
        "currentteam": "currently is on the team {obj}",
        "debutTeam": "debuted with {obj}",
        "draftPick": "was picked #{obj}",
        "draftRound": "was drafted in round {obj}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {obj}",
        "playerNumber": "wears number {obj}",
        "college": "attended {obj}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {obj}",
        "coach": "is coached by {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "city": "is based in {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "anthem": "has the anthem \"{obj}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "has an ethnic group of {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "generalManager": "is managed by {obj}",
        "chairman": "is chaired by {obj}",
        "season": "participated in the season {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "people from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "stadium": "home stadium is {obj}",
        "universityTeam": "university team is {obj}",
        "mostChampions": "has the most championships: {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "country": "is located in {obj}",
        "utcOffset": "has a UTC offset of {obj}",
        "timeZone": "is in the {obj} time zone",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj} square kilometres",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject preserving order of first appearance
    subject_order = []
    subj_groups: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subj_groups:
            subj_groups[t.subject] = []
            subject_order.append(t.subject)
        subj_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    subject_clauses = []
    for subj in subject_order:
        parts = []
        for t in subj_groups[subj]:
            pattern = verb_map.get(t.predicate)
            if pattern:
                # Some patterns need the subject placeholder (e.g., demonym)
                if "{subj}" in pattern:
                    phrase = pattern.format(subj=subj, obj=t.object)
                else:
                    phrase = pattern.format(obj=t.object)
                # Ensure the phrase starts with a lower‑case letter for later joining
                phrase = phrase[0].lower() + phrase[1:] if phrase else phrase
                parts.append(phrase)
            else:
                parts.append(f"{t.predicate} {t.object}")

        # Build the clause for this subject
        if parts:
            # First part gets the subject as the subject of the sentence
            first = parts[0]
            # Capitalise the subject at the beginning
            clause = f"{subj} {first}"
            if len(parts) > 1:
                # Join remaining parts with commas and an "and"
                middle = ", ".join(parts[1:-1])
                if middle:
                    clause += f", {middle}"
                clause += f", and {parts[-1]}"
            subject_clauses.append(clause)

    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""

    # Join different subject clauses with a semicolon for readability
    sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."

    # Capitalise the very first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

                # Join remaining parts with commas and an "and"
                middle = ", ".join(parts[1:-1])
                if middle:
                    clause += f", {middle}"
                clause += f", and {parts[-1]}"
            subject_clauses.append(clause)

    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""

    # Join different subject clauses with a semicolon for readability
    sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."

    # Capitalise the very first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
